rationale: Trump's claimed Iran settlement drove a stock market surge directly affecting US investors and retirement accounts, while Hegseth's Cuba warning and Vance-Netanyahu friction reflect active foreign policy moves with domestic political resonance.

top_events.title: Trump claims US-Iran 'great settlement', markets surge
top_events.summary: Stock markets rallied sharply on news of a claimed Iran deal; directly affects US investors, 401(k)s, and energy prices at the pump.

top_events.title: Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring weapons at Guantánamo visit
top_events.summary: Defense Secretary's Cuba visit signals active US military posturing in the Caribbean; potential policy escalation with domestic political implications.

top_events.title: Vance says Netanyahu 'has got some things wrong'
top_events.summary: Public US-Israel friction from the VP signals a possible shift in Middle East policy stance, relevant to US voters and regional stability.
